mappings.rationaleAI: The Moon's tidal effects on Earth directly address tidal friction (A.2.c) as a dynamic effect related to Earth's rotation.

Al-Biruni's pioneering geodesic measurement of Earth's radius directly addresses the development of improved approximations to Earth's size and shape (B.2) and measurement of geodetic parameters (B.3).
Meteorology covering atmospheric dynamics including the Coriolis force's effect on weather systems addresses the Coriolis force (A.2.a) as related to Earth's rotation.
